athletes.name: Sean Helmot
athletes.training_philosophy: HR-primary, pace secondary. Aerobic base first, sharpen closer to race. Easy runs and long runs Z1-Z2 HR. Quality 1-2x per week. Long run progressive HR blocks.

typical_week.Wed: Quality session (threshold reps or intervals, 10-12km total)
typical_week.Thu: Easy run + drills, gym legs PM (8km Z1-Z2 HR)
typical_week.Fri: Easy run + flat strides (9-10km Z1-Z2 HR)
typical_week.Sat: Easy run — pre-LR activation (6-8km Z1-Z2 HR)

athletes.name: Stacey Harfield
athletes.training_philosophy: HR-capped easy runs, explicit pace targets for quality. Joe Friel 7-zone system. LTHR 180, MaxHR 197.

upcoming.name: Cross Training — 30min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 1

- 30m easy cross training

OPTIONAL — rest is equally appropriate. If moving, swim, aqua jog, or easy bike only. Follow with 15 min stretch — hip flexors and calves. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Easy Jog — 35min HR
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 1

- 35m 65-72% HR

OPTIONAL — if the week has been mostly rest, that is fine. Only run today if legs feel genuinely ready. Walk any time you feel the urge. Heart rate governs pace — not the other way around. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Easy Run — 35min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 2

- 35m Z1 HR

Keep it easy and continuous. Compare how this feels to last week's run/walk — you should notice the legs coming back. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Easy Run — 40min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 2

- 40m Z1 HR

Stepping up 5 minutes from Monday. Heart rate first — slow down before you let it creep up. Coach Helmot.

upcoming.name: Easy Run — 40min
upcoming.description:
Phase: Post-Marathon Recovery — Week 2

- 40m Z1 HR

Third run of the week. Legs should feel noticeably fresher than Week 1. Follow with 10 min stretch. Coach Helmot.

athletes.name: Chess
athletes.training_philosophy: HR-capped easy runs. Joe Friel 7-zone system. LTHR 170, MaxHR 188. London Marathon 2023: 3:10 (4:27/km avg, 164 avg HR).

upcoming.name: Marathon Tempo 10km HR
upcoming.description:
Main set

Marathon specific phase. 10km sustained at marathon effort — Z4 HR throughout.
This is your race effort. Hold 86-90% HR and stay controlled — do not let it drift above 90%.

- 10km 86-90% HR

-- Coach Helmot

athletes.training_philosophy: Triathlon background. Runs 5 days/week. HR-capped easy running, threshold work 1-2x/week. Strong aerobic base from multi-sport history.

athletes.name: Sam
athletes.training_philosophy: Pace-primary quality. LTHR 177, MaxHR 196. HM debut 1:27:25 (4:08/km, 174 avg HR). Strong threshold base — 6x mile @ ~3:58/km. CV capacity 3:45-3:52/km.

upcoming.description:
Main Set

5x

- 3m 5:16-5:28/km Pace

upcoming.name: MSR Hard Fartlek 3x 2m, 1m Jog
upcoming.description:
Main Set

3x
- 2m 3:48-3:53/km Pace
- 1m 3:39-3:43/km Pace
- 30s 3:30-3:34/km Pace
- 2m 4:56-5:06/km Pace

- 3m30s 3:48-3:53/km Pace

Each rep builds through 3 gears -- CV, AP, then a hard 30s VO2 punch. The 2m float is your reset, not a rest. Finish strong with the 3:30 CV. Coach Helmot.

athletes.name: Aidan Burrell
athletes.training_philosophy: Inexperienced runner building base fitness. Conservative pacing, HR-capped easy runs. Focus on consistency and injury prevention.
